summary: Extract item TYPE sub-headers from all 29 pages of the Encyclopedia Magica random-determination tables PDF, so every item can be assigned its type (Oil/Salve/Lens/…) for a correct index page re-join.

pages.confidence: High confidence: this page is the appendix intro plus the Table 1 master d100 category table and the start of Table A: Magical Liquids; the only bold item-TYPE sub-header is "Oils" (first item 63-64 Absinthe). Table 1's rows are category names, not item types, and Table A's opening block has no type header until Oils.

Fairly confident: six clear bold TYPE sub-headers (Oils, Ointments, Perfumes, Philters, Pigments (Paints), Potions) with first rolls read directly beneath each; "Magical Liquids" is treated as the Table A category banner rather than a type, and no explicit "Table A:" banner text is printed on this page so the banner is inferred.

High confidence — all seven sub-headers read cleanly; "Sea Dew" is a single-item type header, and Potions/Salves/Sea Dew/Water fall under Table A (no banner on this page), while Papers/Parchments/Scrolls sit under the visible "Table B: Scrolls" banner.

Fairly confident: two bold sub-headers found — "Scrolls" (continuing from a prior page, no banner visible) and "Enchanted Enhancements*" which sits at roll 01-03 as the first grouping under the "Table C: Rings" banner, with its first following item being 04-07 "of Leadership".

Fairly confident: the two "Table X" banners (D: Rods, E: Staves) and their bold sub-headers (Enchanted Enhancements*, Moto Moto Rods, Rods; and Enchanted Enhancements*, Canes, Gaffs, Staves) are clearly legible, though "Moto Moto Rods" is an unusual header and I read the first rolls carefully against the small print.

High confidence: two bold sub-headers are visible — "Staves" (continuing from a prior page, first item 123-129 of Battle II) and the "Wands" section under the "Table F: Wands" banner (first row 01-08 Enchanted Enhancements*); rolls read carefully from the scan.
High confidence: six sub-headers found (Wands continuation, Books, Librams, Manuals, Maps, Spellbooks); Table G: Books banner is clearly visible; rolls read carefully from the three-column layout.

High confidence on all nine sub-headers and their first rows; Bracelets' first roll prints as "79-180" (likely a scan-clipped "179-180") and Spellbooks/Tomes are continuation headers from the prior Table G page.

High confidence on the 16 sub-headers and their first rolls/items; the only slightly ambiguous case is "Eyepatch of Tenh" (roll 393) which sits between the Eyeglasses list and Gems header as a standalone single-item entry rather than a type sub-header, so I treated it as an item and not a header.

High confidence: all 15 bold TYPE sub-headers across the three columns were captured in reading order; the "Table I: Clothing" banner appears in column 3, so only Capes and Cloaks fall under it while the earlier sections have no banner visible on this page.

pages.confidence: High confidence; all 18 bold TYPE sub-headers were identified in reading order, and the "Table J: Boots, Gloves, and Accessories" banner appears mid-page (below Vestments in column 2), so Anklets onward fall under it while the earlier clothing headers have no banner visible on this page.

High confidence — I read all three columns top-to-bottom and captured all 17 bold TYPE sub-headers; the "Circlets" header first row reads 210-214 "of the Adder" and the Table K banner precedes Belts, though a couple of long fragment item names could have minor transcription nuances.

pages.confidence: High confidence on the sub-headers and rolls; the one judgment call is "Bedroll of Dryness" which is bold but a single-item row (232-235), so I recorded it as its own header.
Very confident: page 14 is a full-page color illustration (battle scene, page number 1580) with no table content or TYPE sub-headers.

High confidence on the sub-headers and roll ranges; two isolated bold items (Coal "of Warmth" and the Cheeses "Coal of Warmth" line) and single-item entries like "Buttercup's Bouquet"/"Bean of Ooze Slime and Jelly" were treated as items not type headers, which is the main judgment call.

Fairly confident on the ten category headers and their roll ranges; note Plumastone (555-556) and single-item bold entries like Eternal Peanut, Fingernail of Rending, Gas of Dreadful Sleep, Grains of Discomfort, Four-Leaf Clover appear to be individual items rather than list headers, so I excluded them, though Plumastone is ambiguous.

High confidence on all sub-headers and roll ranges; the only uncertainty is "Armor Bath" (roll 18-19) which appears as a header with no visible first item row directly beneath it, and "Black Wall" whose roll 108-109 doubles as its own single entry.

High confidence: all three columns read top-to-bottom, left-to-right; distinguished bold category sub-headers (no roll range, heading multiple item rows) from bold single-item entries like "Fishing Lure", "Forge of Metal Protection", "Gauze of Appearance", "Gem Cutter's Tools of Sunndi", "Hook of Adherence", and "Lizardskin Bed" which were excluded; no "Table X" banner appears on this page.

High confidence: all sub-headers read in column order; a couple of out-of-order entries (Rag of Drying, Stylus of Scribing) reflect their actual printed position slightly above the following section header, and single-line device headers (Music Box, Oven of Heating, etc.) have their roll as first_roll with empty first_item.

High confidence — 12 sub-headers captured across all three columns; rolls read directly from the scan, with the Tool Sets header appearing after Torches in the left column and the Table O banner introducing the three instrument-category headers.

High confidence on the 23 sub-headers and their first rolls; slight uncertainty on the exact first roll-range for the single-entry types Brass Horseman (159-161) and Carts (185-186) since their header/row alignment is tight in the middle column.
High — all three columns were legible; I captured every bold TYPE sub-header (Disks through Spheres) and read the first roll-range and item under each; no "Table X" banner appears on this page.

High confidence on the item TYPE sub-headers and their first rolls; the R1/R2/R3 mechanics tables and the R3 "Armor"/"of Valor" internal groupings were intentionally excluded as they are bonus/adjustment charts, not item-type sections.

Moderately high; the five armor-group sub-headers (Armor, Barding, Bonnets, Caparisons, Shield) and the Arrows sub-header under Table S3 are clear, though some roll digits in the dense first column were read carefully from a scan.

Fairly confident on the nine weapon-category headers and their first items; I excluded "Bombard" (row 156) since it reads as a single Blowguns item, not a header with items beneath it. No "Table X" banner is visible on this page (it's the master alphabetical index).

High confidence — all 16 bold TYPE sub-headers across the three columns were read in reading order; rolls verified digit-by-digit, and I excluded bold item names (Discus of Disenchantment, Gnomish Sweeper, Noisome Spirit Chaser) that are not type headers. No "Table X:" banner appears on this page.

High confidence: eight bold TYPE sub-headers are clearly visible (Polearms, Quivers, Shot, Sickles, Slings, Spears, Spelljamming Rams, Swords); no "Table X" banner appears on this page, and the roll digits under each header read cleanly.
High confidence — only one bold TYPE sub-header ("Swords") is present on the page, at the top of the left column, with its first item being roll 732 "Flaming"; no Table banner is visible.

Fairly confident on the three weapon sub-headers (Swords, Throwing Stars, Whips) and the artifact-category sub-headers (Crowns, Orbs, Sceptors); slightly less certain whether "Regalia of Might II" and "Triad of Betrayal" are true bold category sub-headers versus individual artifact-set entries, but they appear formatted as group headers with sub-items beneath them.
